population_milestones.statement: Minimum settlers for long-term survival if Earth resupply stops: time-budget model (labor available vs labor required) with a cooperation sharing factor. "Can it not die."

NSS formal settlement milestone: 1,000+ adults. The full NSS criterion also requires a Mars-born generation that has itself had viable children; modeled here as population-only until demographics exist (see docs/CITY_RAMP_RESEARCH.md section 1).
population_milestones.source: NSS Space Settlement Roadmap milestone 25, 3-0 verified

population_milestones.statement: Order-of-magnitude population for full industrial closure — needs nothing from Earth ever again. Handmer's analogy: Earth's full industrial stacks each rest on 10-100M people; Mars needs less diversity but still ~1M. Musk quotes the same number without a shown derivation.
population_milestones.source: Handmer 2017 roadmap (verified by direct fetch); field survey spans 1e5-1e9

power_subsistence_kwe.source: Vazquez Pombo, Space: Science & Technology 2021, verified verbatim from the DTU repository PDF: "24 or 25 kW/person for missions ranging from 4 to 500 people"; Table 1: 25/25/23/20/17 kWe/person at stages 0-4. Note: literature-carried figure endorsed in a peer-reviewed venue; its 30-200 kWe "ISRU" is life-support-scale, NOT a propellant factory.

power_industrial_kwe.source: Same source's industrial-city regime — order-of-magnitude above subsistence. Do not conflate the two regimes.

food_area_m2.source: NASA BVAD TP-2015-218570/REV2 Table 4-92 totals row, verified verbatim (19.50 / 65.29 m2 per crew member); Cannon & Britt 46 m2 (2-1 verified); Frontiers ~40-50 m2 couples food to O2/CO2 closure.

habitable_volume_m3.source: NASA/NTRS 20200002973 verified verbatim: minimum NHV 115.83 m3 / 4 crew (28.96) and 170.14 / 6 (28.36 — flat with crew size); realistic cylindrical design 147.19 / 4 = 36.80 (+27% for access/structure).

pressurized_volume_m3.source: Same study: 306 m3 total pressurized for 4 crew (NHV + systems + stowage). Habitat catalog components should be checked against this, not against NHV alone.

consumables_kg_per_day.source: NASA BVAD verified verbatim ("Oxygen Consumed kg/CM-d 0.895", food "kg/CM-d 0.800", "Potable Water Content kg/CM-d 3.217"). This is the zero-closure import floor: ~1.8 t/person/year before any recycling.

no_local_production_t_per_person_year.source: Handmer make-buy analysis (verified by direct fetch): ~10 t at ~$2M per person-year

raw_materials_closed_kg_per_person_year.source: Same source: 'perhaps 20 kg' once basic-commodity production is onshored (~500x cliff)

make_local_below_usd_per_kg.source: Handmer's rule: <$100/kg local production cost always beats importing

import_mass.electronics_closure_warning: Tier B (arxiv 1612.03238, 3-0 verified): incomplete closure of electronics/chips makes import mass grow EXPONENTIALLY with the industry — the decay curve has a floor until Gen 5.0-6.0 closure.

fleet_min_growth_per_synod.source: New Space 2022 (PMC9527650), 3-0 verified: "total number of landed vehicles to double at a minimum with each consecutive opportunity." The ONLY ships-per-synod rule that survived adversarial verification; every Musk cadence figure was refuted or superseded.

handmer_doubling.source: Handmer 2017: double per window, ~10x per decade (his own label: ambitious)

city_npc_conservative.source: arxiv 2112.06155 (verified from PDF text 2026-07-10): self-described conservative import ramp

closure_sequence.source: arxiv 1612.03238 (Freitas/Gilbreath lineage, 3-0 verified); consistent with Handmer's oxygen->water/fuel->plastics->metals->electronics->chips ordering and NSS's minimum set (life support, food, local construction).
